feedback.text: Wouldn't it be great to also include video games which have already been sold to me (the customer). I fear that many games that are 'online only', meaning they run on servers which I have no control over, could be shut down tomorrow if they don't generate enough revenue for the publishers. Many titles on the market right now which people pay a lot of money for have no secure future, because there is no 'end of life' plan for them. I am of the opinion that if I buy a product for ~ 80 today, I want to be able to use it forever, because i BOUGHT it. If a publisher can theoretically pull the plug at any time, then I feel like I got scammed. I paid them and they delivered a product which does not work, because the seller flipped a 'now its broken' switch. This feels more like I'm RENTING a product, but even then, I have no clue for how long. So if you could please cover video games under this initiative, I would be very thankful. Also, here is a list of games that are already 'dead', because the publishers pulled the metaphorical plug: https://stopkillinggames.wiki.gg/wiki/Dead_game_list
